item.content:encoded:
Dr. Choi: There is no pizza, is there!?

I can HEAR the pain in this person’s voice and it made me laugh out loud. Like, the betrayal of it. How dare it offer a nonexistent pizza!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I told you personally, but "Shut the door, this guy is really extra. I don't want to listen to him." is probably the best line of dialogue on the wiki.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Almost passed out not even a quarter of the way through the logs…

Came for the

SCP-2317-JK: CURSE YOU FOUNDATION! I'LL GET YOU NEXT TIME!

Stayed for the

SCP-2317-JK: FUCK YOU TOO, PAL! I COULD END THE WORLD WITHOUT RAISING A SWEAT IF I COULD JUST FIT THROUGH THIS DAMN DOOR!

+50/10, would read again if I thought I could survive the lack of oxygen!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought that Clef's own deconstruction in the discussion of the original 2317 was funnier.

That said yours still funny in it's own right and deconstructs an aspect of the original that Clef's attempt missed (Clef pointed out that it wouldn't be a threat to us if it broke through to our world, you pointed out that it wouldn't be able to break through to our world in the first place), so I'm upvoting regardless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That bit has taught me a valuable lesson: never read a -J article at work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Serious!Butt-ghost would either end up terrifying or stupid (probably stupid unfortunately).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
AND I WOULD HAVE GOTTEN AWAY WITH IT TOO, IF IT HADN'T BEEN FOR YOU AND YOUR LITTLE DOG!

I`m dead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really appreciate the brevity of the description. It is a stark contrast with the original article, almost like a subversion since it is known for how long it gets to explain the real treat. Also it makes sense with the tone of the article, because the dangers are really obvious, stated upfront, but people keep forgetting about it and always want to open the door!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, devouring the world, ending civilization, causing the eschaton, you know. Typical Monday.

LMAO that had me dead +1

woop woop, second to break +100

here's a reading that hasn't be linked yet in this thread

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Scarlet King's Pies

Oh, fuck you.

+1

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
I died laughing, job well done, and I hope this audio adaptation does it justice:

https://youtu.be/wZ8FVHhzyBc

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In some cultures, monsters only attack when they are invited to a place. Supposedly, they teach the moral of not letting strangers in one's home.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It didn't make me laugh, but it was very amusing reading about a world ending entity who can't do shit unless someone literally opens a door for him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't forget SCP-173-J and SCP-682-J.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2317-JK: GOD BLESS IT.

I feel like that sums up my feelings on this entire thing. This is goddamn great. +1 my dog.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This genuinely made me laugh. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Off the top of my head, SCP-231-J, SCP-076-J and SCP-1797-J are also parodies of mainlisters.

EDIT: Also SCP-3999-J and SCP-049-J.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes! Then I can finally post Habil and Qabil! If there was a parody contest I'd feel it would at least stand a chance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, slang I don't get isn't a problem. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked it, but a couple notes:

  • The last log takes away more than it adds. I loved the idea that it's the sticky note every time that prevents the end of the world, but the last log renders this pointless and makes the previous entries unimportant to the narrative.
  • The "you and your little dog" was kind of awkwardly placed and too much of a reference for my taste.

+1, but with reservations

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Description is my least favorite part of the article - I feared that if I made the Description too long, it would probably spoil the jokes of the logs, which was where I had already put most of the humor. I'm going to see if I can make a better Description.

Extra isn't a typo, it's slang for over the top or excessibely dramatic. It's not really a word an O5 would use.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
RA Vance, Sgt. Masterson, Dr. Choi, Dr. Fellini: Gasp!

I was already grinning throughout the article, but it was this line that threw me into a fit of laughter. Take your +1-J.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe it could also include a category for serious counterparts to existing joke SCPs? There are a lot of great -J's that could also work incredibly well as mainlisters, sans sillyness.

For instance, things attacking from inside a toilet or drain is a legitimate, semi-common phobia: serious!butt-ghost could be terrifying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is an amusing parade of stupidity.

My favorite line:

I ACTUALLY MADE A PIZZA TO TRICK THEM ONE TIME!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted, but I'm disappointed SCP-2317-J didn't say "AAH! AFTER TEN THOUSAND YEARS I'M FREE! IT'S TIME TO CONQUER EARTH!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm impressed at how long you kept the joke up with it still being funny.

SCP-2317-JK: I AM THE BLACKBOX-BLACKBOX-BLACKBOX-BLACKBOX

WHY IS THIS HILARIOUS

AND THE SCOOBY-DOO JOKE ALMOST KILLED ME

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ehhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhh I don't straight up dislike it, but it way overstays its welcome. The first three logs were essentially the same, and while the fourth one was at least amusing it didn't help that the rest was so repetitive. Like I said, just hung around longer than it needs to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has me asking this: Should we have a contest wherein we make affectionate parodies of pre-existing SCPs? There seems to be an untapped oil well here, perhaps?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agree that it needs some ironing out, but I love the premise- and the logs really do shine through for this one. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It uses the idea of people being stupid to be funny. And it works. I have to say, though, I really liked the use of in-universe avatar Kain Pathos Crow. In-universe avatars live on. 'No important casualties' HE IS DEAD.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1.Do you really think the doorway is the only possible way to get to and from that universe?

2. The Foundation has encountered a number of entities capable of interdimensional travel. It wouldn't surprise me if SCP-2317-K had that ability.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This actually does make some sense. The original SCP-2317 never explains how it is that they expect a gigantic demonic creature to fit though a normal sized door, or why they don't just destroy the door or move it off of the planet or something like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmmm

On the plus side, the logs were rather amusing. Right from the guy nonchalantly checking the Erikesh codex I knew it was going to be good.

On the downside, you've still got a lot of spelling/grammar issues (it's/its problems, for example) and maybe typos (he's very extra?). Also, the stuff before the log is pretty bland/blunt. You end up summarizing a lot of 2317 quickly/sloppily so you can get on to the joke. That hurts this a lot because the story you're cranking down is some of the most elaborately written and built up stuff on the wiki.

No vote. This could be solid with some tweaking, but making a parody of an article like this means that the stuff at the top will need more thought, or maybe be trimmed down. I was a little surprised there wasn't any messing around with tabs, but that's not super necessary.

item.title: I'm sorry, Dr. Clef
item.content:encoded:
This is a parody of Dr. Clef's SCP-2317. It's probably not absolutely necessary to have read that first, but a lot of the context is drawn from it and you should read it. This also butchers the tone of that article.

RA Vance, Sgt. Masterson, Dr. Choi, Dr. Fellini, Kain Pathos Crow and O5-13 are the six people that log into the different iterations of SCP-2317. The D-Class are original.

Thanks to the following for looking this over in draft form:

  • DrLycus
  • DrChandra

TheeSherm did an audio recording of this, which can be found here! Check it out!

The opinions voiced by O5-13 in the footnote were sourced from Panteradactyl and Ensophos.

If you liked this thing and would like to read some comments about it, or check out other things I've written, head to my author page for more!
